name: DNA Repair Enhancement coverage repair assessment for June 10, 2026

assessment_window.basis: Coverage repair created after the June 10 surveillance assessment requested systemic repair-regulator, NAD/nucleotide boundary, active-registry, recent-review, and safety/off-target repair before ordinary surveillance resumed.

summary: Coverage is now adequate for the current low-confidence human_biomarker_signal public claim, pending review and publication of the staged repair bundle. The repair stages SIRT6 cross-species DSB-repair evidence, OGG1 small-molecule repair activation in cells, and a recent DNA repair targeting review, while recording NAD/nucleotide, photoprotection, telomere, endothelial disease-mechanism, and ataxia-telangiectasia registry hits as boundary context. Coverage is not strong because systemic human DNA repair endpoints, durable safety, and repair-regulator target engagement in people remain absent.

evidence_categories.rationale: The baseline already covers oral nicotinamide skin-cancer chemoprevention, topical DNA repair enzyme or photolyase skin studies, and indirect NR/NMN human trials. Coverage repair rechecked close nicotinamide, NMN, NR, and ataxia-telangiectasia registries and found no posted systemic human DNA repair endpoint results.
evidence_categories.gap_note: This remains an evidence gap, not a coverage gap: direct systemic human DNA repair endpoint trials are still missing.

evidence_categories.rationale: The public map contains human association context through DREAM/Alzheimer disease, and the repair reviewed endothelial DNA damage and nucleotide/telomere boundary records, but human observational coverage is not yet deeply mapped and is not central to the current intervention-oriented claim.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Future stronger coverage could map human repair-capacity cohorts, progeroid syndromes, and disease-specific DNA damage biomarkers.

evidence_categories.rationale: The staged SIRT6 record repairs the named preclinical repair-regulator gap with cross-species longevity-linked DSB repair evidence, while the published DREAM record adds mutation-burden and cross-mammal lifespan association context.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Coverage is not strong because drug-like animal healthspan interventions for DNA repair enhancement remain sparse.

evidence_categories.rationale: The repaired map covers skin UV DNA damage, nicotinamide repair-proximal biology, indirect NAD biology, DREAM repression, SIRT6 DSB repair, OGG1 BER activation, and review-level DNA repair pathway targeting.
The ONTRANS null trial, NR/NMN no-DNA-repair-endpoint findings, no-results registry checks, and review-level context-dependence all remain visible as limiting evidence.

The staged review explicitly reinforces context-specific repair-state targeting and the need to distinguish repair restoration from repair addiction or oncologic DDR manipulation, but no chronic systemic repair-enhancement safety or durability evidence exists in people.
evidence_categories.gap_note: This remains an evidence gap requiring future result-bearing repair-regulator studies, not another immediate source-completeness pass.

evidence_categories.rationale: Coverage repair rechecked the named nicotinamide, NMN, NR, NADage, and ataxia-telangiectasia registries. No direct DNA repair endpoint results were posted; NADage, NMN middle-aged/elderly, and NMN exercise-tolerance records are already represented under NAD and Redox Restoration.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Watch NAD and disease-boundary registries during ordinary surveillance only if they post direct DNA repair endpoints or repair-regulator results.

evidence_categories.rationale: The staged PMID 42227145 repair-targeting review repairs recent review triangulation for pathway, biomarker, safety, and taxonomy boundaries; the repair also reviewed but excluded NAD homeostasis, photoprotection, DNA glycosylase/AD, and biological photoprotection reviews as adjacent context.

The repaired map now includes skin-local human evidence, ONTRAC/ONTRANS clinical anchors, DREAM repression, SIRT6 DSB repair, OGG1 activation, and recent review triangulation. This is adequate for the current cautious public claim.
evidence_categories.gap_note: A formal citation-network audit could support a future strong verdict but is not needed before ordinary surveillance resumes.

evidence_categories.rationale: The repair keeps SIRT6 and OGG1 in DNA Repair Enhancement because they directly modulate repair biology, keeps NAD/NMN/NR registries primarily under NAD and Redox Restoration unless direct repair endpoints appear, keeps nucleotide/telomere records as boundary context, and keeps ataxia-telangiectasia NR registries as disease-specific no-results context.

search_log_summary: Reviewed the published DNA Repair Enhancement baseline and DREAM surveillance update; searched PubMed for recent repair reviews, SIRT6/DREAM/glycosylase/ATM/ATR repair-regulator sources, NAD/nucleotide boundary records, and direct human DNA repair endpoint trials; checked ClinicalTrials.gov for the named nicotinamide, NMN, NR, NADage, and ataxia-telangiectasia records; and searched historical DNA repair enzyme/photolyase/T4N5 skin registries.
source_selection_notes: PMID 31002797 was staged because it directly links SIRT6-mediated DSB repair to longevity-linked cross-species repair capacity.
PMID 35737787 was staged because it directly tests small-molecule activation of OGG1 and oxidative DNA damage repair in cells.
PMID 42227145 was staged because it repairs recent-review and safety/taxonomy boundary coverage for DNA repair pathway targeting.
PMID 42265534 was excluded from public staging because its older-adult nucleotide result used DNAmAge and telomere endpoints rather than direct DNA repair enhancement.
NCT06592859, NCT07144527, and NCT06208527 remain NAD/redox trial-watch records unless direct DNA repair endpoints or linked repair-regulator results are posted.

known_gaps.description: No result-bearing systemic human intervention study was found with direct DNA repair endpoints in aging-relevant tissues beyond skin-local UV damage settings.
known_gaps.suggested_action: During ordinary surveillance, watch for systemic human repair-regulator target-engagement studies before changing the stage.

known_gaps.description: DREAM, SIRT6, OGG1, and broader DDR targeting remain preclinical, cellular, comparative, or review-level; chronic systemic safety, durability, off-target genome-response, and cancer-risk boundaries are not established in people.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Keep public wording conservative until repair-regulator programs show safe target engagement and aging-relevant endpoints in humans.

known_gaps.description: The repair covers obvious SIRT6, OGG1, DREAM, skin-local, NAD-boundary, registry, and review anchors, but no formal citation-network audit has been recorded.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Run a deeper citation-network or must-include audit only if a future public stage change is contemplated.

next_coverage_action: Run evidence review and editorial review for the staged coverage-repair bundle. If approved and published, return DNA Repair Enhancement to ordinary surveillance with special watch on systemic human repair-regulator target engagement, posted NAD/NMN/NR repair endpoints, and safety or off-target genome-response signals.
